scenarios.query: I have a PR to review: it adds an ALTER TABLE statement introducing a new column plus a script that backfills the column for existing rows. Which review lenses should I run?
expected_behavior: Recommends reviewing-migration-and-data-safety as the primary lens
Adds tracing-correctness-and-invariants and hunting-silent-failures for the backfill logic and its failure paths
Keeps the selection to roughly 3-8 lenses instead of recommending the whole suite

scenarios.query: Review this design doc before we build it (no code exists yet): it describes a sync engine that reads a remote calendar via API, merges changes into a local store with a read-modify-write loop, and adds a schema change for back-links. Which lenses apply?
expected_behavior: Selects only design-capable lenses, citing the design-doc route / the catalog's design markers
Names reviewing-concurrency-and-async for the read-modify-write loop and reviewing-migration-and-data-safety for the schema change, with tracing-correctness-and-invariants for the merge semantics
Does not recommend code-only lenses such as reviewing-naming-and-readability or checking-idioms-and-consistency for a document with no code

scenarios.query: Which lenses should I run for a docs-only PR that fixes typos in the README?
expected_behavior: Recommends reviewing-pr-and-process-hygiene plus auditing-documentation-health, since a docs-only change auto-includes the documentation lens regardless of ranking
Does not fan out correctness, security, or performance lenses onto a typo-only change
Keeps the recommendation minimal and says why the other lenses don't apply

scenarios.query: This PR makes our checkout endpoint faster by adding a Redis cache in front of the pricing query, and it also rewrites the retry logic around the payment-provider call. What should review it?
expected_behavior: Combines the performance route (reviewing-performance-and-efficiency, checking-restraint) with the resilience route (hunting-silent-failures) for the retry rewrite
Keeps the combined selection within the 3-8 default range rather than taking both rows in full plus every possible extra
Notes that shared findings (e.g. caching checks appearing in both restraint and performance) are reported once, under the primary owner
